name: Cortex
what_it_does: Engineering intelligence platform with a REST API for service scorecards, catalog management, and on-call integration to measure and improve engineering quality.
use_cases: Measure engineering maturity across services using customizable scorecards and pass/fail initiatives
Query the service catalog to retrieve ownership, dependencies, and health metadata for any service
Automate service governance by triggering workflows when scorecard thresholds are breached
Integrate on-call data from PagerDuty and Opsgenie to correlate incidents with service quality
not_for: Teams with fewer than 10 services where spreadsheets suffice for tracking ownership
Organizations not using GitHub, GitLab, or standard DevOps tooling that Cortex integrates with
best_when: Best when engineering leaders need quantified visibility into service health and maturity across a large, distributed team.
avoid_when: Avoid when budget is constrained and the free tier's 10-service cap is insufficient.

auth.notes: API key passed as Bearer token in Authorization header. Keys are per-organization with no scope granularity documented in public API.

pricing.paid_tiers: Paid plans from $500/month; enterprise pricing on request
pricing.notes: Free tier is very limited at 10 services. Most production use cases require paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits not publicly documented; contact sales for high-volume API usage

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns standard HTTP status codes with JSON error bodies; detail level is adequate but not exceptional.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Service catalog upserts are idempotent by service tag; scorecard evaluations are read-only and safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: No official SDK means agents must implement HTTP calls directly
Rate limits are undocumented, making it difficult to tune retry backoff
Scorecard definitions must exist before agents can query them; creation is UI-driven

security_score_components.security_notes: API key auth is simple but lacks scope granularity. SOC 2 compliant. TLS enforced on all endpoints.
